title: JD Vance Flew to Switzerland Today to Cut an Iran Nuclear Deal
script: Iran and the US — talking? Today, Vice President JD Vance landed at the Bürgenstock Resort on Lake Lucerne, Switzerland, for nuclear ceasefire talks with Iran. He's joining Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Pakistan and Qatar are in the room too. The goal: turn the US-Iran memorandum of understanding into a permanent peace deal. The Strait of Hormuz is currently open — Trump said no tolls during the 60-day ceasefire. These talks were almost canceled just days ago after a snag. They revived. If they hold, it's the biggest US-Iran agreement since 1979. Lebanon is on the table too. So: if the US and Iran actually sign a permanent deal, what does that change?
